 {"id":"wa-fykv","title":"FTUI-03.2.a Route in-process logs through single output sink","description":"## Background
In-process output must obey one-writer constraints to keep rendering deterministic and avoid output corruption.

## Deliverables
- inventory and removal plan for direct stdout/stderr writes in UI-active paths
- unified output sink routing contract for logs/events/status lines
- verification checks to prove no bypass writes remain

## Acceptance Criteria
- all targeted paths route through the sanctioned sink while UI is active
- checks detect and fail on new bypass writes
- validation evidence includes unit assertions and runtime logs demonstrating routing correctness.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:05.996650629Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.260744-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:12:15.49917289Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-fykv","depends_on_id":"wa-m76j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-g08v","title":"Quick-fix tests: template interpolation, error remediation coverage, UX validation","description":"
# Quick-Fix Testing Suite

## Purpose
Ensure all suggestions are accurate, copy-pasteable, and helpful.

## Test Categories

### 1. Template Interpolation Tests
- {pane} substitution works
- {event_id} substitution works
- Invalid templates fail gracefully

### 2. Error Remediation Coverage
- Every WaError variant has remediation
- Remediations are non-empty
- Commands are syntactically valid

### 3. UX Validation Tests
- Commands can be copy-pasted (no smart quotes, etc.)
- Commands are actually runnable
- Suggestions match actual system state

### 4. Context-Aware Tests
- Typo detection finds correct suggestions
- Available resources are accurate
- Platform detection is correct

### 5. Snapshot Tests
- Error message format stability
- Suggestion format stability

## Test Fixtures
- Error scenarios with known remediations
- Event scenarios with known suggestions
- Platform-specific command sets

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% of error types have remediation tests
- [ ] 100% of rule types have suggestion tests
- [ ] Snapshot tests for output stability
- [ ] Copy-paste validation (no encoding issues)

## Testing
- Unit tests for template rendering and suggested_action interpolation.
- Integration tests: fixture errors/events produce actionable suggestions.
- E2E: extend an events/error scenario to assert suggestions and rule-id logs.

 {"id":"wa-g2l","title":"[EPIC] Phase 4: Polish & Integration","description":"# Phase 4: Polish & Integration

## Overview
This epic focuses on user experience, external integrations, and robustness. After Phase 3, wa works - now we make it delightful to use and integrate with the broader ecosystem.

## Strategic Importance
Phase 4 transforms wa from "working prototype" to "production-ready tool":
- MCP server enables AI agents to control wa
- rich_rust output makes CLI pleasant for humans
- Setup automation reduces friction for new users
- CASS integration enables session archaeology
- Comprehensive testing ensures reliability

## Key Components

### 1. MCP Server with fastmcp_rust
Tools:
- wa.state - Get current pane states
- wa.get_text - Get text from pane
- wa.send - Send text to pane
- wa.wait_for - Wait for pattern
- wa.search - FTS search
- wa.events - Get events
- wa.workflow_run - Execute workflow
- wa.accounts - List accounts
- wa.accounts_refresh - Refresh account usage
- wa.rules_list - List detection rules
- wa.rules_test - Test pattern matching

Resources:
- wa://panes - Current pane registry
- wa://events - Event feed
- wa://accounts - Account status
- wa://workflows - Available workflows
- wa://rules - Pattern rules

### 2. rich_rust CLI Output
- render_pane_table() - Tables for pane listings
- render_event_panel() - Panels for events with severity colors
- Progress indicators for long operations
- Consistent styling across all commands

### 3. Setup Automation

#### SSH Config Parser
- Parse ~/.ssh/config
- Extract hosts, hostnames, users, ports, identity files
- Filter out wildcards

#### Automated Remote Setup
1. Check if wezterm is installed
2. Install WezTerm if needed (apt/dnf)
3. Create systemd service for wezterm-mux-server
4. Enable lingering for service persistence
5. Optionally install wa binary
6. Verify service is running

#### WezTerm Config Generator
- Generate ssh_domains with WezTerm multiplexing
- Domain-specific colors (optional)
- wa integration hooks (user-var-changed forwarding)
- Idempotent patching (WA-BEGIN/WA-END markers)

### 4. CASS Integration (coding_agent_session_search)
- find_session(session_id) - Correlate wa session with cass data
- search_sessions(path, agent) - Find sessions in a directory
- Session archaeology for context recovery
- Token usage correlation

### 5. Comprehensive Testing

#### Unit Tests
- Delta extraction correctness (overlap cases, wrap cases)
- Pattern rules per agent (positive + negative fixtures)
- Workflow step guards (prompt detection, alt-screen checks)
- DB schema migration tests
- Policy engine capability checks

#### Integration Tests
- Simulate wezterm cli outputs via fixtures
- Run workflow engine against fake panes
- Test StorageHandle async operations

#### Golden Corpus Regression Tests
Directory structure:
\`\`\`
tests/corpus/
├── codex/
│   ├── usage_limit_v1.txt
│   ├── usage_limit_v1.expect.json
├── claude_code/
│   ├── session_end.txt
│   └── session_end.expect.json
└── gaps/
    ├── scrollback_truncation.txt
    └── scrollback_truncation.expect.json
\`\`\`

#### Property-Based Tests (proptest)
- Segment sequence monotonicity
- FTS finds inserted text
- Delta extraction completeness

#### Performance Budgets (Criterion)
- pattern_detection_typical: p50 < 1ms, p99 < 5ms
- fts_query_common: p50 < 10ms, p99 < 50ms
- CI enforcement with baseline comparison

## Success Criteria
- [ ] MCP server exposes all robot mode functionality
- [ ] CLI output is beautiful with rich_rust
- [ ] \`wa setup\` configures local and remote hosts
- [ ] CASS integration works for session correlation
- [ ] Test coverage > 70% overall
- [ ] All performance budgets met
- [ ] Documentation sufficient for contributors

## Dependencies
- Depends on Phase 1-3 (all core functionality)

## Configuration
At this point, wa.toml should be fully functional:

\`\`\`toml
[general]
log_level = "info"
data_dir = "~/.local/share/wa"

[ingest]
poll_interval_ms = 200
gap_detection = true

[storage]
db_path = "~/.local/share/wa/wa.db"
retention_days = 30

[patterns]
packs = ["builtin:core", "builtin:codex", "builtin:claude_code"]

[workflows]
enabled = ["handle_compaction", "handle_usage_limits"]
max_concurrent = 3

[safety]
rate_limit_per_pane = 30
require_prompt_active = true

[metrics]
enabled = false
bind = "127.0.0.1:9464"
\`\`\`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:48:07.901261212Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:10.578560397Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:10.578560397Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of wa-nu4.3 (Phase 4: Polish & Integration). wa-nu4.3 has correct priority (P1) and dependency chain."}

 {"id":"wa-g4xb","title":"Docs: timing determinism guidelines (no sleeps; quiescence patterns)","description":"# Task: Document timing determinism guidelines

## Goal
Prevent flakiness from re-entering the codebase by documenting best practices.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to use wait-for vs quiescence
  - how to set timeouts and backoff
  - how to write actionable timeout errors
  - explicit "no fixed sleep" rule + exceptions policy

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contributors can follow a short checklist to write deterministic tests.

 {"id":"wa-g7p2","title":"Human CLI tests: output layer snapshots + no-ANSI guarantees","description":"# Task: Human CLI tests for output layer

## Goal
Make the human CLI output layer trustworthy and regression-resistant.

Humans will pipe wa output into files, paste it into issues, and rely on it under stress. Output regressions are user-hostile.

## Scope

### Renderer-level snapshot tests (core renderers)
Renderer-level tests (no subprocess) for the **Phase 4 baseline** human CLI:
- status/panes table renderer
- events list/panel renderer (**raw/technical** event fields)
- query/search results renderer
- workflow summary renderer
- audit feed renderer

### Explicit non-scope (covered elsewhere)
To keep priorities and dependencies sane, this bead intentionally does **not** block on Phase 4 “UX excellence extras”. Those renderers/overlays must still be tested, but they are covered by their own beads:
- Natural-language event descriptions in event rendering: `wa-0go.7`
- History timeline / rollback visualization output: `wa-5em.9`
- Suggestion overlays in status/events/errors: `wa-tp4.8`

This separation keeps the **core CLI output layer** stable early, while allowing optional overlays to evolve without blocking core ship readiness.

## Format selection behavior
- auto mode chooses rich only when explicitly told "tty" (injectable in tests)
- plain mode is stable and contains no ANSI escape sequences
- json mode is stable and uses a documented schema (may share the robot envelope)

## Key requirements
- Snapshot tests for plain output (golden text). The exact formatting should be intentional.
- Snapshot tests for json output (stable fields, stable error codes).
- Redaction expectations:
  - never print secrets in plain/rich output
  - when showing inputs, use a redacted summary consistent with audit rules

## Implementation notes
- Keep renderers pure: typed input -> String.
- Avoid dependence on real TTY detection in tests. Inject a bool or OutputMode instead.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A deliberate formatting change requires updating snapshots.
- Piped output contains no ANSI sequences by default.
- Errors follow the same structure across commands (message + hint + code).

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add an explicit assertion that plain-mode output contains no `\x1b[` ANSI escapes.
  - Add a fake secret string in inputs and assert it never appears in renderer output.

- Snapshot discipline:
  - Snapshots should be per-renderer and small enough to review; avoid one mega-snapshot that is hard to update.

 {"id":"wa-ggw9w","title":"Make Lua optional in FrankenTerm config crate (feature-flag)","description":"# Make Lua Optional in FrankenTerm Config Crate

## Context
The config crate (frankenterm/config/) hard-depends on mlua with features=[vendored, lua54, async, send, serialize]. Under the new dual-runtime strategy (Option C), Lua becomes optional — users can choose Lua, WASM, or both.

## What changes
1. Make mlua dependency optional behind `lua` feature
2. Make luahelper dependency optional behind `lua` feature  
3. Gate all Lua-related code in config/src/ behind `#[cfg(feature = "lua")]`
4. Default features INCLUDE `lua` for backward compat
5. `no-lua` feature excludes it (for minimal/headless builds)
6. Ensure config crate compiles and provides basic config loading even without Lua

## Feature flag design
```toml
[features]
default = ["lua"]
lua = ["dep:mlua", "dep:luahelper"]
no-lua = []  # Explicit opt-out
```

## Files affected
- frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml (feature flags, optional deps)
- frankenterm/config/src/lua.rs (gate behind #[cfg(feature = "lua")])
- frankenterm/config/src/lib.rs (conditional module inclusion)
- Any file in config/src/ that imports from lua.rs

## Why keep Lua as default
Existing WezTerm users have wezterm.lua configs. Breaking them by default would be hostile. The `lua` feature is default-on, `no-lua` is opt-in for minimal builds.

## Testing
- `cargo check -p config` — must work (default, with Lua)
- `cargo check -p config --no-default-features` — must compile (no Lua)
- `cargo check -p config --features lua` — explicit Lua, must work
- `cargo test -p config` — existing tests pass

## Cross-References
- **wa-3k2g1** (luahelper optional): Once config gates its luahelper dep behind `lua`, the luahelper crate becomes truly optional at the workspace level. This bead is a prerequisite for wa-3k2g1.
- **wa-2ufom** (Mux Lua optional): The mux crate depends on config. Mux's Lua optionality depends on config's Lua optionality being done first — otherwise mux inherits a transitive hard dependency on mlua through config.
- Together with wa-3k2g1 and wa-2ufom, this forms the "Lua-optional triad" — all three must land for a clean no-Lua build path.","notes":"Landed on origin/main (commit 54226a1). Added lua feature (default-on) + optional mlua/luahelper deps; cfg-gated Lua modules + conversion macros; added no-lua stubs so --no-default-features builds. Verified: cargo check -p config --all-targets; cargo check -p config --all-targets --no-default-features; cargo test (workspace) passes with TMPDIR=/Users/jemanuel/tmp-ft. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings currently fails in multiple workspace crates (pre-existing).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkElk","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:47.712922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:58:11.886079-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:58:11.886083-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ggw9w","depends_on_id":"wa-3dfxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:56.164581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-ggw9w","depends_on_id":"wa-2i2vi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:56.294002Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
